state_dict_confirmation: checkpoint object is an OrderedDict state_dict
pytorch_forward_order_confirmed: reshape NCHW to [B, C, H*W] and mean over spatial axis
rearrange 'b p w -> w p b'

stage0.proj: Identity; no projection weights are used for stage 0

stage1.proj: Identity; no projection weights are used for stage 1

stage2.proj: Identity; no projection weights are used for stage 2

stage3.proj: Identity; no projection weights are used for stage 3

math: repeat each feature row num_bins times to emulate grouped Conv1d expansion

heads.class_head: Dense(1) + sigmoid, mapped from PyTorch class_head
heads.class_head2: Dense(1) + sigmoid, mapped from PyTorch class_head2
heads.cls_head: Present in PyTorch state_dict but unused by DAT.forward and intentionally not used in TF forward.

drift_impact_interpretation: Accumulated Stage G-2/G-3 feature drift did not materially affect final scores under preferred thresholds.
warnings: stage_h_full_dat_parity.py:132: You are using `torch.load` with `weights_only=False` (the current default value), which uses the default pickle module implicitly. It is possible to construct malicious pickle data which will execute arbitrary code during unpickling (See https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/blob/main/SECURITY.md#untrusted-models for more details). In a future release, the default value for `weights_only` will be flipped to `True`. This limits the functions that could be executed during unpickling. Arbitrary objects will no longer be allowed to be loaded via this mode unless they are explicitly allowlisted by the user via `torch.serialization.add_safe_globals`. We recommend you start setting `weights_only=True` for any use case where you don't have full control of the loaded file. Please open an issue on GitHub for any issues related to this experimental feature.
